name: schema-action:ai.generateText
input:
{"prompt":"You are a form schema generator. Given a description of what a form should collect, design a concrete form schema with appropriate fields.

User's form description: ## Purpose
##Purpose
Create a patient intake form for our orthopedic clinic with insurance details and medical history focused on musculoskeletal conditions.

##Audience
Patients visiting the orthopedic clinic for the first time.

##Constraints
Must capture insurance provider and policy details. Pain assessment using a standardized scale is required.

Analyze the description to identify:
- What data needs to be collected
- Who will fill out the form and in what context
- What field types and options are most appropriate
- What validation or constraints apply

Design the form fields using these types:
- "select" for choices with defined options (include options in validation.options)
- "string" for free text
- "number" for quantities
- "boolean" for yes/no
- "date" for dates
- "email" for email addresses

RULES:
- Field keys MUST be camelCase and descriptive
- "description" should be SHORT (a few words) — omit entirely if the label already makes the field obvious
- "tooltip" is for extra guidance that helps the user fill in the field correctly — omit if not needed"}

us?","type":"select","required":false,"key":"referralSource"}]}, "configuratorFormValues":[{"key":"allowSaveAndResume","value":true},{"key":"requireSignatureOnSubmit","value":true},{"key":"autoSaveIntervalSeconds","value":60},{"key":"showProgressBar","value":true}]}

name: design-probe-action:ai.generateText.doGenerate
input:
[{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"You are a form design assistant. Generate 3-5 refinement questions to improve this form design. Each question has 2-4 options.

User's form description: ## Purpose
##Purpose
Collect intake information from new fitness coaching clients including their health history, fitness goals, and availability.

##Audience
New clients signing up for personal training or group fitness coaching.

##Exclusions
Do not collect detailed medical records — only relevant health conditions that affect exercise safety.

Each question must be classified by layer:
- "intent": about the form's purpose, audience, or high-level goals
- "dimensions": about specific aspects of data collection, field choices, or structure
- "both": spans both intent and concrete form structure

Rules:
- Question "text" must be VERY SHORT — max ~8 words, like a headline. No preamble.
- "explanation" is a brief subheadline that adds context ONLY when the headline isn't self-explanatory. Keep it to one sentence.
- Option labels must be SHORT: 2-5 words max, mutually exclusive
- Mix of intent-level and dimension-level questions
- Values should be camelCase identifiers"}]}]

name: schema-action:ai.generateText.doGenerate
input:
[{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"You are a form schema generator. Given a description of what a form should collect, design a concrete form schema with appropriate fields.

User's form description: ## Purpose
##Purpose
Create a patient intake form for our orthopedic clinic with insurance details and medical history focused on musculoskeletal conditions.

##Audience
Patients visiting the orthopedic clinic for the first time.

##Constraints
Must capture insurance provider and policy details. Pain assessment using a standardized scale is required.

Analyze the description to identify:
- What data needs to be collected
- Who will fill out the form and in what context
- What field types and options are most appropriate
- What validation or constraints apply

Design the form fields using these types:
- "select" for choices with defined options (include options in validation.options)
- "string" for free text
- "number" for quantities
- "boolean" for yes/no
- "date" for dates
- "email" for email addresses

RULES:
- Field keys MUST be camelCase and descriptive
- "description" should be SHORT (a few words) — omit entirely if the label already makes the field obvious
- "tooltip" is for extra guidance that helps the user fill in the field correctly — omit if not needed"}]}]

These are duplicate fields — the first also has a malformed name with a trailing comma, which will likely cause a data mapping or submission error.","fieldIds":["field-1774623253678-5","field-1774623253678-6"],"fixes":[{"value":"remove_field_5","label":"Remove malformed duplicate","description":"Delete field-1774623253678-5 (the one with the trailing comma in its name) and keep field-1774623253678-6 which has the correct name 'biologicalSex'."},{"value":"fix_name_field_5","label":"Fix name, merge fields","description":"Correct the name of field-1774623253678-5 from 'biologicalSex,' to 'biologicalSex' and remove field-1774623253678-6, ensuring only one field remains."},{"value":"rename_differentiate","label":"Rename if intentionally different","description":"If the two fields are intentionally distinct (e.g., gender identity vs biological sex), rename and relabel them clearly to reflect their separate purposes."}]},{"kind":"missing_required","severity":"warning","description":"'healthConditionsDetails' (field-1774623253678-12) is not required, but it is the only way to capture detail when 'hasRelevantHealthConditions' is true. For a health and safety intake form, leaving this optional risks coaches receiving a 'yes' answer with no supporting detail, which is a safety concern.","fieldIds":["field-1774623253678-11","field-1774623253678-12"],"fixes":[{"value":"conditional_required","label":"Make conditionally required","description":"Set healthConditionsDetails as required when hasRelevantHealthConditions is true, using conditional/dependent field logic."},{"value":"add_validation_note","label":"Add placeholder warning","description":"Keep the field optional but add a description warning clients that details are strongly recommended if they answered yes, to prompt completion."},{"value":"merge_into_one","label":"Merge into single field","description":"Replace the boolean and detail fields with a single text field asking clients to describe any health conditions, or write 'None', eliminating the conditional dependency."}]},{"kind":"missing_required","severity":"warning","description":"'preferredTrainingLocation' (field-1774623253678-19) is required, but 'hasGymAccess' (field-1774623253678-18) is also required and directly constrains which locations are valid. If a client has no gym access, requiring them to select a gym-based location creates a logical conflict. There is no conditional logic linking these two fields.","fieldIds":["field-1774623253678-18","field-1774623253678-19"],"fixes":[{"value":"conditional_location","label":"Make location conditional","description":"Show or filter preferredTrainingLocation options based on the answer to hasGymAccess — e.g., hide 'Gym' as an option if the client answered 'No'."},{"value":"remove_gym_access","label":"Remove redundant boolean","description":"Remove hasGymAccess entirely and infer gym access from the preferredTrainingLocation selection, reducing redundancy."},{"value":"add_description_link","label":"Add cross-field guidance","description":"Add a description to preferredTrainingLocation instructing clients to select only locations they have access to, making the dependency explicit without conditional logic."}]},{"kind":"type_mismatch","severity":"warning","description":"'emailAddress' (field-1774623253678-2) and 'phoneNumber' (field-1774623253678-3) are both typed as plain 'text' with no constraints. Email and phone fields should use specialised types or format constraints to enable validation, preventing malformed contact details that would make the client unreachable.","fieldIds":["field-1774623253678-2","field-1774623253678-3"],"fixes":[{"value":"use_email_type","label":"Use email/tel types","description":"Change emailAddress to type 'email' and phoneNumber to type 'tel' so browsers and validators enforce basic format checking natively."},{"value":"add_regex_constraints","label":"Add regex constraints","description":"Keep type as 'text' but add regex pattern constraints (e.g., RFC 5322 for email, E.164 or local format for phone) to validate input on submission."},{"value":"add_descriptions","label":"Add format hint descriptions","description":"At minimum, add description hints (e.g., 'Enter a valid email address' / 'Include country code if outside UK') to guide clients toward correct input, reducing data quality issues."}]},{"kind":"missing_required","severity":"info","description":"'injuriesOrLimitations' (field-1774623253678-20) is optional, but for a personal training intake form, undisclosed injuries are a significant safety and liability risk. Unlike additional notes, this field has direct implications for programme design and client safety.","fieldIds":["field-1774623253678-20"],"fixes":[{"value":"make_required_with_none","label":"Require with 'None' option","description":"Make the field required and instruct clients to write 'None' if they have no injuries, ensuring coaches always receive an explicit answer rather than silence."},{"value":"convert_to_boolean_pair","label":"Add boolean + detail pair","description":"Add a preceding boolean field 'Do you have any current or recent injuries?' and make the detail field conditionally required when answered yes, mirroring the health conditions pattern."},{"value":"add_strong_description","label":"Strengthen field description","description":"Keep optional but update the description to 'Important: describe any current or recent injuries or physical limitations. name: schema-action:ai.generateText.doGenerate

[{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"You are a form schema generator. Given a description of what a form should collect, design a concrete form schema with appropriate fields.

User's form description: ## Purpose
##Purpose
Collect intake information from new fitness coaching clients including their health history, fitness goals, and availability.

##Audience
New clients signing up for personal training or group fitness coaching.

##Exclusions
Do not collect detailed medical records — only relevant health conditions that affect exercise safety.

Analyze the description to identify:
- What data needs to be collected
- Who will fill out the form and in what context
- What field types and options are most appropriate
- What validation or constraints apply

Design the form fields using these types:
- "select" for choices with defined options (include options in validation.options)
- "string" for free text
- "number" for quantities
- "boolean" for yes/no
- "date" for dates
- "email" for email addresses

RULES:
- Field keys MUST be camelCase and descriptive
- "description" should be SHORT (a few words) — omit entirely if the label already makes the field obvious
- "tooltip" is for extra guidance that helps the user fill in the field correctly — omit if not needed"}]}]

name: design-probe-action:ai.generateText.doGenerate
input:
[{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"You are a form design assistant. Generate 3-5 refinement questions to improve this form design. Each question has 2-4 options.

User's form description: ## Purpose
##Purpose
Create a patient intake form for our orthopedic clinic with insurance details and medical history focused on musculoskeletal conditions.

##Audience
Patients visiting the orthopedic clinic for the first time.

##Constraints
Must capture insurance provider and policy details. Pain assessment using a standardized scale is required.

Each question must be classified by layer:
- "intent": about the form's purpose, audience, or high-level goals
- "dimensions": about specific aspects of data collection, field choices, or structure
- "both": spans both intent and concrete form structure

Rules:
- Question "text" must be VERY SHORT — max ~8 words, like a headline. No preamble.
- "explanation" is a brief subheadline that adds context ONLY when the headline isn't self-explanatory. Keep it to one sentence.
- Option labels must be SHORT: 2-5 words max, mutually exclusive
- Mix of intent-level and dimension-level questions
- Values should be camelCase identifiers"}]}]

{"prompt":"You are a form schema generator. Given a description of what a form should collect, design a concrete form schema with appropriate fields.

User's form description: ## Purpose
##Purpose
Create a rental agreement form for construction equipment. Need to capture equipment details, rental period, pricing, insurance requirements, and operator certification.

##Audience
Construction company managers renting heavy equipment (excavators, cranes, bulldozers) from our fleet.

##Constraints
Must comply with OSHA equipment operation requirements. Insurance documentation is mandatory.

Analyze the description to identify:
- What data needs to be collected
- Who will fill out the form and in what context
- What field types and options are most appropriate
- What validation or constraints apply

Design the form fields using these types:
- "select" for choices with defined options (include options in validation.options)
- "string" for free text
- "number" for quantities
- "boolean" for yes/no
- "date" for dates
- "email" for email addresses

RULES:
- Field keys MUST be camelCase and descriptive
- "description" should be SHORT (a few words) — omit entirely if the label already makes the field obvious
- "tooltip" is for extra guidance that helps the user fill in the field correctly — omit if not needed"}

1. **semantic_overlap**: Fields that capture the same information differently (e.g., "email" and "contactEmail")

3. **contradictory_constraints**: Constraints that conflict with each other or the field type
4. **naming_inconsistency**: Mix of naming conventions (e.g., camelCase and snake_case) or unclear labels
5. **missing_required**: Fields that should logically be required but aren't, given the form's purpose

If a user says they have no gym access, a 'gym' option in preferredTrainingLocation becomes contradictory and confusing.","fieldIds":["field-1774623080454-22","field-1774623080454-23"],"fixes":[{"value":"remove:hasGymAccess","label":"Remove gym access boolean","description":"Drop 'hasGymAccess' and infer gym access from the 'preferredTrainingLocation' selection instead."},{"value":"conditional:preferredTrainingLocation","label":"Make location field conditional","description":"Keep both fields but show 'preferredTrainingLocation' only when 'hasGymAccess' is true, making the relationship explicit."}]},{"kind":"missing_required","severity":"info","description":"'injuryOrLimitationDetails', 'chronicHealthConditionDetails', and 'medicationDetails' are optional even when their paired boolean fields are true. name: design-probe-action:ai.generateText
input:
{"prompt":"You are a form design assistant. Generate 3-5 refinement questions to improve this form design. Each question has 2-4 options.

User's form description: ## Purpose
##Purpose
Create a patient intake form for our orthopedic clinic with insurance details and medical history focused on musculoskeletal conditions.

##Audience
Patients visiting the orthopedic clinic for the first time.

##Constraints
Must capture insurance provider and policy details. Pain assessment using a standardized scale is required.

Each question must be classified by layer:
- "intent": about the form's purpose, audience, or high-level goals
- "dimensions": about specific aspects of data collection, field choices, or structure
- "both": spans both intent and concrete form structure

Rules:
- Question "text" must be VERY SHORT — max ~8 words, like a headline. No preamble.
- "explanation" is a brief subheadline that adds context ONLY when the headline isn't self-explanatory. Keep it to one sentence.
- Option labels must be SHORT: 2-5 words max, mutually exclusive
- Mix of intent-level and dimension-level questions
- Values should be camelCase identifiers"}
output: {"interactions":[{"text":"What is the primary visit reason?","explanation":"Helps tailor which musculoskeletal history fields to prioritize.","layer":"intent","options":[{"value":"newInjury","label":"New injury"},{"value":"chronicCondition","label":"Chronic condition"},{"value":"postSurgeryFollowUp","label":"Post-surgery follow-up"},{"value":"generalConsult","label":"General consultation"}]},{"text":"Which pain scale should be used?","explanation":"Determines the standardized pain assessment format included in the form.","layer":"dimensions","dimensionName":"Pain Assessment Scale","options":[{"value":"numericRatingScale","label":"Numeric (0–10)"},{"value":"visualAnalogScale","label":"Visual analog scale"},{"value":"wongBakerFaces","label":"Wong-Baker Faces"},{"value":"brief painInventory","label":"Brief Pain Inventory"}]},{"text":"How detailed should insurance capture be?","explanation":"Defines how many insurance-related fields to include.","layer":"dimensions","dimensionName":"Insurance Details","options":[{"value":"basicOnly","label":"Provider & policy only"},{"value":"includeSecondary","label":"Include secondary insurance"},{"value":"fullVerification","label":"Full verification fields"}]},{"text":"Should the form be completed digitally or on paper?","explanation":"Affects field layout, signature capture, and accessibility considerations.","layer":"both","options":[{"value":"digitalOnly","label":"Digital only"},{"value":"paperOnly","label":"Paper only"},{"value":"both","label":"Both formats"}]},{"text":"Which body regions should history cover?","explanation":"Scopes the musculoskeletal history section to relevant anatomical areas.","layer":"dimensions","dimensionName":"Medical History Scope","options":[{"value":"fullBody","label":"Full body"},{"value":"spineAndJoints","label":"Spine & joints only"},{"value":"patientSelected","label":"Patient-selected regions"}]}]}

name: detect-conflicts:ai.generateText
input:
{"prompt":"You are a form schema quality analyzer. Detect conflicts, inconsistencies, and issues in this form schema.

Form intent: ## Purpose
This form is a customer self-checkout interface for booking heavy machinery rentals, covering service selection, machine reservation, scheduling, and payment. Customers fill in their contact details manually with no account login required. Rental duration options are tailored to the selected machine type, and customers can set up recurring rentals with a chosen frequency — capped at a maximum of 12 months from the booking date. Machine units are identified by type and ID for unambiguous selection, and the form is designed for clarity and customer autonomy throughout.

Schema fields:
[
  {
    "id": "field-1774573849802-0",
    "name": "firstName",
    "label": "First Name",
    "type": "text",
    "required": true,
    "constraints": []
  },
  {
    "id": "field-1774573849802-1",
    "name": "lastName",
    "label": "Last Name",
    "type": "text",
    "required": true,
    "constraints": []
  },
  {
    "id": "field-1774573849802-2",
    "name": "emailAddress",
    "label": "Email Address",
    "type": "text",
    "required": true,
    "constraints": []
  },
  {
    "id": "field-1774573849802-3",
    "name": "phoneNumber",
    "label": "Phone Number",
    "type": "text",
    "required": false,
    "constraints": []
  },
  {
    "id": "field-1774573849802-5",
    "name": "serviceType",
    "label": "Service Type",
    "type": "select",
    "required": true,
    "constraints": []
  },
  {
    "id": "field-1774573849803-6",
    "name": "machineId",
    "label": "Select Machine",
    "type": "select",
    "required": true,
    "description": "Choose a specific unit to reserve",
    "constraints": []
  },
  {
    "id": "field-1774573849803-7",
    "name": "bookingDate",
    "label": "Booking Date",
    "type": "date",
    "required": true,
    "constraints": []
  },
  {
    "id": "field-1774573849803-8",
    "name": "rentalDuration",
    "label": "Rental Duration",
    "type": "select",
    "required": true,
    "description": "Options vary by machine type",
    "constraints": []
  },
  {
    "id": "field-1774573849803-9",
    "name": "isRecurring",
    "label": "Recurring Booking?",
    "type": "boolean",
    "required": true,
    "constraints": []
  },
  {
    "id": "field-1774573849803-10",
    "name": "recurrenceFrequency",
    "label": "Repeat Frequency",
    "type": "select",
    "required": false,
    "constraints": []
  },
  {
    "id": "field-1774573849803-11",
    "name": "recurrenceEndDate",
    "label": "Recurrence End Date",
    "type": "date",
    "required": false,
    "constraints": []
  },
  {
    "id": "field-1774573849803-13",
    "name": "jobSiteAddress",
    "label": "Job Site Address",
    "type": "text",
    "required": true,
    "description": "Where the machine will be used",
    "constraints": []
  },
  {
    "id": "field-1774573849803-14",
    "name": "operatorRequired",
    "label": "Operator Needed?",
    "type": "boolean",
    "required": true,
    "description": "Include a certified operator",
    "constraints": []
  },
  {
    "id": "field-1774573849803-15",
    "name": "paymentMethod",
    "label": "Payment Method",
    "type": "select",
    "required": true,
    "constraints": []
  },
  {
    "id": "field-1774573849803-16",
    "name": "billingAddress",
    "label": "Billing Address",
    "type": "text",
    "required": true,
    "constraints": []
  },
  {
    "id": "field-1774573849803-17",
    "name": "specialInstructions",
    "label": "Special Instructions",
    "type": "text",
    "required": false,
    "description": "Additional notes for your booking",
    "constraints": []
  }
]

Look for these issues (only report REAL problems, not minor style preferences):
1. **semantic_overlap**: Fields that capture the same information differently (e.g., "email" and "contactEmail")
2. **type_mismatch**: Field type doesn't match its label/description (e.g., a "date of birth" stored as text)
3. **contradictory_constraints**: Constraints that conflict with each other or the field type
4. **naming_inconsistency**: Mix of naming conventions (e.g., camelCase and snake_case) or unclear labels
5. **missing_required**: Fields that should logically be required but aren't, given the form's purpose

For EACH conflict found, provide 2-3 concrete fix options (not generic advice).
If no conflicts found, return an empty conflicts array.
Rules:
- Only real issues, not nitpicks
- Max 5 conflicts
- Fix labels must be SHORT (2-6 words)
- severity: "error" for breaking issues, "warning" for quality issues, "info" for suggestions"}
output: {"conflicts":[{"kind":"type_mismatch","severity":"error","description":"emailAddress is typed as plain 'text' with no format constraint. An email field should use type 'email' to enable browser-level validation and prevent malformed addresses from being submitted.","fieldIds":["field-1774573849802-2"],"fixes":[{"value":"change type to 'email'","label":"Use email field type","description":"Change the field type from 'text' to 'email' so browsers and validators enforce proper email format automatically."},{"value":"add constraint: pattern '^[^@\\s]+@[^@\\s]+\\.[^@\\s]+$'","label":"Add regex pattern constraint","description":"Keep type as 'text' but add a regex pattern constraint to validate email format on submission."},{"value":"add constraint: format 'email'","label":"Add format constraint","description":"Keep type as 'text' but add a format constraint of 'email' to signal validators to apply email-specific rules."}]},{"kind":"missing_required","severity":"warning","description":"phoneNumber is optional, but for a heavy machinery rental booking with no account login, a phone number is a critical contact fallback. If email delivery fails or urgent scheduling changes arise, there is no secondary contact method.","fieldIds":["field-1774573849802-3"],"fixes":[{"value":"set required: true on phoneNumber","label":"Make phone required","description":"Mark phoneNumber as required so every booking has at least two contact methods, reducing failed communication risk."},{"value":"add constraint: requireAtLeastOne ['emailAddress','phoneNumber']","label":"Require one contact method","description":"Keep phoneNumber optional but add a cross-field constraint requiring at least one of emailAddress or phoneNumber to be provided."},{"value":"add description: 'Recommended for urgent scheduling updates'","label":"Add guidance description","description":"Keep it optional but add a description nudging customers to provide a phone number, improving voluntary completion rate."}]},{"kind":"missing_required","severity":"error","description":"recurrenceFrequency and recurrenceEndDate are not required, but the form intent states recurring rentals have a chosen frequency capped at 12 months. If isRecurring is true, both fields are logically mandatory — leaving them optional means incomplete recurring bookings can be submitted.","fieldIds":["field-1774573849803-10","field-1774573849803-11"],"fixes":[{"value":"add conditionalRequired: { when: 'isRecurring', is: true } to both fields","label":"Conditionally require both fields","description":"Mark recurrenceFrequency and recurrenceEndDate as conditionally required when isRecurring equals true, preventing incomplete recurring booking submissions."},{"value":"add constraint: maxDateOffset '+12months' from bookingDate on recurrenceEndDate and set required conditionally","label":"Require with 12-month cap","description":"Make both fields conditionally required when isRecurring is true, and add a maxDateOffset constraint on recurrenceEndDate to enforce the 12-month cap from bookingDate."},{"value":"merge into single 'recurrenceConfig' composite field","label":"Merge into composite field","description":"Replace both fields with a single composite recurrenceConfig field (frequency + endDate) that is conditionally required when isRecurring is true, simplifying schema logic."}]},{"kind":"missing_required","severity":"warning","description":"recurrenceEndDate has no constraint linking it to bookingDate or enforcing the 12-month maximum stated in the form intent. A customer could enter an end date in the past or more than 12 months ahead with no validation.","fieldIds":["field-1774573849803-11","field-1774573849803-7"],"fixes":[{"value":"add constraint: minDate = bookingDate, maxDate = bookingDate + 12 months","label":"Add date range constraint","description":"Add a minDate constraint equal to bookingDate and a maxDate constraint of bookingDate plus 12 months on recurrenceEndDate to enforce the stated cap."},{"value":"add constraint: maxDateOffset '12months' relative to bookingDate","label":"Add 12-month offset cap","description":"Add a relative maxDateOffset constraint of 12 months from bookingDate so the cap is dynamically calculated regardless of when the form is filled."},{"value":"add description warning and backend validation","label":"Document and validate server-side","description":"Add a description to recurrenceEndDate stating the 12-month limit and enforce it with server-side validation as a minimum safeguard."}]},{"kind":"semantic_overlap","severity":"warning","description":"jobSiteAddress and billingAddress are both free-text address fields with no relationship defined between them. Many customers will have identical addresses, yet there is no 'same as job site' option, leading to duplicate data entry and potential inconsistency if one is updated.","fieldIds":["field-1774573849803-13","field-1774573849803-16"],"fixes":[{"value":"add boolean field 'billingAddressSameAsJobSite' that auto-copies jobSiteAddress when true","label":"Add same-address toggle","description":"Introduce a boolean toggle that, when checked, copies jobSiteAddress into billingAddress, reducing duplicate entry and inconsistency."},{"value":"add constraint: copyFrom 'jobSiteAddress' when billingAddressSameAsJobSite is true","label":"Link fields conditionally","description":"Keep both fields but add a conditional copy constraint so billingAddress is pre-filled from jobSiteAddress and only editable when the customer explicitly opts for a different billing address."},{"value":"add description to billingAddress: 'Leave blank to use job site address'","label":"Default to job site address","description":"Make billingAddress optional with a description indicating it defaults to jobSiteAddress if left blank, reducing friction while preserving flexibility."}]}]}

User's form description: ## Purpose
##Purpose
Create a rental agreement form for construction equipment. Need to capture equipment details, rental period, pricing, insurance requirements, and operator certification.

##Audience
Construction company managers renting heavy equipment (excavators, cranes, bulldozers) from our fleet.

##Constraints
Must comply with OSHA equipment operation requirements. Insurance documentation is mandatory.

Analyze the description to identify:
- What data needs to be collected
- Who will fill out the form and in what context
- What field types and options are most appropriate
- What validation or constraints apply

Design the form fields using these types:
- "select" for choices with defined options (include options in validation.options)
- "string" for free text
- "number" for quantities
- "boolean" for yes/no
- "date" for dates
- "email" for email addresses

RULES:
- Field keys MUST be camelCase and descriptive
- "description" should be SHORT (a few words) — omit entirely if the label already makes the field obvious
- "tooltip" is for extra guidance that helps the user fill in the field correctly — omit if not needed"}

This will likely cause data mapping errors and confuse users who see the same question twice.","fieldIds":["field-1774623253678-5","field-1774623253678-6"],"fixes":[{"value":"remove_field-1774623253678-5","label":"Remove comma-named duplicate","description":"Delete field 'field-1774623253678-5' (the one with the trailing comma in its name) and keep the clean 'biologicalSex' field."},{"value":"remove_field-1774623253678-6","label":"Remove second duplicate","description":"Delete field 'field-1774623253678-6' and fix the name of 'field-1774623253678-5' by removing the trailing comma, keeping only one Biological Sex field."},{"value":"merge_into_one","label":"Merge into single field","description":"Consolidate both into a single field with name 'biologicalSex', label 'Biological Sex', type 'select', and required true."}]},{"kind":"naming_inconsistency","severity":"warning","description":"Field 'field-1774623253678-5' has a trailing comma in its name value ('biologicalSex,'), which is an invalid field name. A user who answers 'No' to gym access would logically not select 'Gym' as preferred location, but there is no conditional logic or constraint linking these fields, allowing contradictory responses.","fieldIds":["field-1774623253678-18","field-1774623253678-19"],"fixes":[{"value":"make_location_conditional","label":"Conditionally show location field","description":"Show 'preferredTrainingLocation' only when 'hasGymAccess' is true, or filter its options based on the boolean answer to avoid contradictions."},{"value":"remove_boolean_keep_select","label":"Remove boolean, keep select","description":"Remove 'hasGymAccess' entirely and infer gym access from the 'preferredTrainingLocation' answer, reducing redundancy."},{"value":"add_cross_field_validation","label":"Add cross-field validation","description":"Add a constraint that flags an error if 'preferredTrainingLocation' is 'Gym' while 'hasGymAccess' is false."}]},{"kind":"missing_required","severity":"info","description":"'emailAddress' is stored as a plain 'text' type with no format constraint. For a fitness coaching intake form, an invalid email address would prevent the coach from contacting the client, making email format validation effectively required for the form to be useful.","fieldIds":["field-1774623253678-2"],"fixes":[{"value":"change_type_to_email","label":"Change type to email","description":"Change the field type from 'text' to 'email' so the browser and backend enforce a valid email format natively."},{"value":"add_regex_constraint","label":"Add email regex constraint","description":"Keep the type as 'text' but add a regex constraint (e.g., pattern matching RFC 5322) to validate the email format on submission."},{"value":"add_format_description","label":"Add format hint to label","description":"At minimum, add a description like 'Must be a valid email address' and handle validation server-side to reduce invalid submissions."}]}]}

name: design-probe-action:ai.generateText
input:
{"prompt":"You are a form design assistant. Generate 3-5 refinement questions to improve this form design. Each question has 2-4 options.

User's form description: ## Purpose
##Purpose
Collect intake information from new fitness coaching clients including their health history, fitness goals, and availability.

##Audience
New clients signing up for personal training or group fitness coaching.

##Exclusions
Do not collect detailed medical records — only relevant health conditions that affect exercise safety.

Each question must be classified by layer:
- "intent": about the form's purpose, audience, or high-level goals
- "dimensions": about specific aspects of data collection, field choices, or structure
- "both": spans both intent and concrete form structure

Rules:
- Question "text" must be VERY SHORT — max ~8 words, like a headline. No preamble.
- "explanation" is a brief subheadline that adds context ONLY when the headline isn't self-explanatory. Keep it to one sentence.
- Option labels must be SHORT: 2-5 words max, mutually exclusive
- Mix of intent-level and dimension-level questions
- Values should be camelCase identifiers"}
output: {"interactions":[{"text":"Who completes this form?","explanation":"Understanding who fills it out affects tone and field complexity.","layer":"intent","options":[{"value":"clientAlone","label":"Client independently"},{"value":"clientWithCoach","label":"Client with coach"},{"value":"coachOnBehalf","label":"Coach on client's behalf"}]},{"text":"How will health conditions be collected?","explanation":"Determines whether to use a checklist, free text, or a hybrid approach.","layer":"dimensions","dimensionName":"Health History","options":[{"value":"checkboxList","label":"Checkbox list"},{"value":"freeText","label":"Open text field"},{"value":"checkboxPlusFreeText","label":"Checklist + free text"}]},{"text":"How specific should fitness goals be?","layer":"intent","options":[{"value":"broadCategories","label":"Broad categories only"},{"value":"specificTargets","label":"Specific measurable targets"},{"value":"bothLevels","label":"Both broad and specific"}]},{"text":"How should availability be captured?","explanation":"Affects whether clients pick days, times, or session frequency.","layer":"dimensions","dimensionName":"Availability","options":[{"value":"dayOfWeek","label":"Days of week"},{"value":"timeSlots","label":"Specific time slots"},{"value":"sessionFrequency","label":"Sessions per week"},{"value":"allOfAbove","label":"All of the above"}]},{"text":"Should the form gate on a liability waiver?","explanation":"Decides if consent must be collected before intake questions appear.","layer":"both","options":[{"value":"waiverFirst","label":"Waiver before form"},{"value":"waiverAtEnd","label":"Waiver at submission"},{"value":"noWaiver","label":"No waiver needed"}]}]}

Using a plain text type means no format validation occurs, allowing malformed email addresses to be submitted on a legal rental agreement where contact accuracy is critical.","fieldIds":["field-1774623189482-2"],"fixes":[{"value":"change_to_email_type","label":"Change type to email","description":"Set the field type to 'email' so the browser and form engine enforce standard email format validation automatically."},{"value":"add_regex_constraint","label":"Add email regex constraint","description":"Keep type as 'text' but add a pattern constraint such as /^[^\\s@]+@[^\\s@]+\\.[^\\s@]+$/ to enforce email format at the validation layer."},{"value":"rename_and_retype","label":"Rename and retype field","description":"Rename the field to 'businessEmailAddress', change type to 'email', and add a description clarifying it must be a valid corporate email address."}]},{"kind":"missing_required","severity":"warning","description":"'Delivery Site Address' (field-1774623189482-18) is not required, but 'Delivery Required' (field-1774623189482-17) is a required boolean. input:
{"prompt":"You are a form design assistant. Generate 3-5 refinement questions to improve this form design. Each question has 2-4 options.

User's form description: ## Purpose
##Purpose
Collect intake information from new fitness coaching clients including their health history, fitness goals, and availability.

##Audience
New clients signing up for personal training or group fitness coaching.

##Exclusions
Do not collect detailed medical records — only relevant health conditions that affect exercise safety.

Each question must be classified by layer:
- "intent": about the form's purpose, audience, or high-level goals
- "dimensions": about specific aspects of data collection, field choices, or structure
- "both": spans both intent and concrete form structure

Rules:
- Question "text" must be VERY SHORT — max ~8 words, like a headline. No preamble.
- "explanation" is a brief subheadline that adds context ONLY when the headline isn't self-explanatory. Keep it to one sentence.
- Option labels must be SHORT: 2-5 words max, mutually exclusive
- Mix of intent-level and dimension-level questions
- Values should be camelCase identifiers"}

{"prompt":"You are a form schema generator. Given a description of what a form should collect, design a concrete form schema with appropriate fields.

User's form description: ## Purpose
##Purpose
Create a patient intake form for our orthopedic clinic with insurance details and medical history focused on musculoskeletal conditions.

##Audience
Patients visiting the orthopedic clinic for the first time.

##Constraints
Must capture insurance provider and policy details. Pain assessment using a standardized scale is required.

Analyze the description to identify:
- What data needs to be collected
- Who will fill out the form and in what context
- What field types and options are most appropriate
- What validation or constraints apply

Design the form fields using these types:
- "select" for choices with defined options (include options in validation.options)
- "string" for free text
- "number" for quantities
- "boolean" for yes/no
- "date" for dates
- "email" for email addresses

- Field keys MUST be camelCase and descriptive
- "description" should be SHORT (a few words) — omit entirely if the label already makes the field obvious
- "tooltip" is for extra guidance that helps the user fill in the field correctly — omit if not needed"}

For a health and safety intake form, leaving this optional risks coaches receiving a 'yes' answer with no supporting detail, which is a safety concern.","fieldIds":["field-1774623253678-11","field-1774623253678-12"],"fixes":[{"value":"conditional_required","label":"Make conditionally required","description":"Set healthConditionsDetails as required when hasRelevantHealthConditions is true, using conditional/dependent field logic."},{"value":"add_validation_note","label":"Add placeholder warning","description":"Keep the field optional but add a description warning clients that details are strongly recommended if they answered yes, to prompt completion."},{"value":"merge_into_one","label":"Merge into single field","description":"Replace the boolean and detail fields with a single text field asking clients to describe any health conditions, or write 'None', eliminating the conditional dependency."}]},{"kind":"missing_required","severity":"warning","description":"'preferredTrainingLocation' (field-1774623253678-19) is required, but 'hasGymAccess' (field-1774623253678-18) is also required and directly constrains which locations are valid. There is no conditional logic linking these two fields.","fieldIds":["field-1774623253678-18","field-1774623253678-19"],"fixes":[{"value":"conditional_location","label":"Make location conditional","description":"Show or filter preferredTrainingLocation options based on the answer to hasGymAccess — e.g., hide 'Gym' as an option if the client answered 'No'."},{"value":"remove_gym_access","label":"Remove redundant boolean","description":"Remove hasGymAccess entirely and infer gym access from the preferredTrainingLocation selection, reducing redundancy."},{"value":"add_description_link","label":"Add cross-field guidance","description":"Add a description to preferredTrainingLocation instructing clients to select only locations they have access to, making the dependency explicit without conditional logic."}]},{"kind":"type_mismatch","severity":"warning","description":"'emailAddress' (field-1774623253678-2) and 'phoneNumber' (field-1774623253678-3) are both typed as plain 'text' with no constraints. name: schema-action:ai.generateText
input:
{"prompt":"You are a form schema generator. Given a description of what a form should collect, design a concrete form schema with appropriate fields.

User's form description: ## Purpose
##Purpose
Collect intake information from new fitness coaching clients including their health history, fitness goals, and availability.

##Audience
New clients signing up for personal training or group fitness coaching.

##Exclusions
Do not collect detailed medical records — only relevant health conditions that affect exercise safety.

Analyze the description to identify:
- What data needs to be collected
- Who will fill out the form and in what context
- What field types and options are most appropriate
- What validation or constraints apply

Design the form fields using these types:
- "select" for choices with defined options (include options in validation.options)
- "string" for free text
- "number" for quantities
- "boolean" for yes/no
- "date" for dates
- "email" for email addresses

RULES:
- Field keys MUST be camelCase and descriptive
- "description" should be SHORT (a few words) — omit entirely if the label already makes the field obvious
- "tooltip" is for extra guidance that helps the user fill in the field correctly — omit if not needed"}

{"prompt":"You are a form design assistant. Generate 3-5 refinement questions to improve this form design. Each question has 2-4 options.

User's form description: ## Purpose
##Purpose
Create a rental agreement form for construction equipment. Need to capture equipment details, rental period, pricing, insurance requirements, and operator certification.

##Audience
Construction company managers renting heavy equipment (excavators, cranes, bulldozers) from our fleet.

##Constraints
Must comply with OSHA equipment operation requirements. Insurance documentation is mandatory.

Each question must be classified by layer:
- "intent": about the form's purpose, audience, or high-level goals
- "dimensions": about specific aspects of data collection, field choices, or structure
- "both": spans both intent and concrete form structure

Rules:
- Question "text" must be VERY SHORT — max ~8 words, like a headline. No preamble.
- "explanation" is a brief subheadline that adds context ONLY when the headline isn't self-explanatory. Keep it to one sentence.
- Option labels must be SHORT: 2-5 words max, mutually exclusive
- Mix of intent-level and dimension-level questions
- Values should be camelCase identifiers"}
output: {"interactions":[{"text":"Who completes this form?","explanation":"Determines field complexity and terminology level.","layer":"intent","options":[{"value":"rentersOnly","label":"Renters only"},{"value":"ourStaffOnly","label":"Our staff only"},{"value":"both","label":"Both parties"}]},{"text":"How is operator certification verified?","explanation":"Affects whether cert fields are self-reported or require document upload.","layer":"dimensions","dimensionName":"Operator Certification","options":[{"value":"selfDeclared","label":"Self-declared checkbox"},{"value":"uploadDoc","label":"Upload certificate"},{"value":"licenseNumber","label":"Enter license number"},{"value":"thirdPartyVerify","label":"Third-party verification"}]},{"text":"How is rental pricing structured?","explanation":"Determines which pricing fields and calculations to include.","layer":"dimensions","dimensionName":"Pricing","options":[{"value":"dailyRate","label":"Daily rate only"},{"value":"tieredRates","label":"Tiered daily/weekly/monthly"},{"value":"quoteBased","label":"Quote-based approval"},{"value":"depositPlusRate","label":"Deposit plus rate"}]},{"text":"Should insurance be submitted upfront?","explanation":"Decides if insurance docs are required before form submission or collected later.","layer":"both","options":[{"value":"requiredUpfront","label":"Required at submission"},{"value":"withinGracePeriod","label":"Within grace period"},{"value":"verifiedExternally","label":"Verified externally"}]},{"text":"Is equipment condition documented at handoff?","explanation":"Determines if pre/post rental inspection fields are needed in the form.","layer":"dimensions","dimensionName":"Equipment Details","options":[{"value":"photoUpload","label":"Photo upload"},{"value":"checklistForm","label":"Condition checklist"},{"value":"notIncluded","label":"Separate process"}]}]}

Create a patient intake form for our orthopedic clinic with insurance details and medical history focused on musculoskeletal conditions.

##Audience
Patients visiting the orthopedic clinic for the first time.

##Constraints
Must capture insurance provider and policy details. Pain assessment using a standardized scale is required.

Each question must be classified by layer:
- "intent": about the form's purpose, audience, or high-level goals
- "dimensions": about specific aspects of data collection, field choices, or structure
- "both": spans both intent and concrete form structure

Rules:
- Question "text" must be VERY SHORT — max ~8 words, like a headline. No preamble.
- "explanation" is a brief subheadline that adds context ONLY when the headline isn't self-explanatory. Keep it to one sentence.
- Option labels must be SHORT: 2-5 words max, mutually exclusive
- Mix of intent-level and dimension-level questions
- Values should be camelCase identifiers"}
